message:
perf: single-pass normalization, SSE zero-copy, regex OnceLock, dedup memchr

Four targeted performance improvements across the codebase:

src/tools.rs — normalize_for_match():
- Replace 3 chained String-allocating normalize calls (unicode spaces,
  quotes, dashes) with a single-pass char iterator that normalizes all
  three categories in one allocation via match arms
- Eliminates 2 intermediate String allocations per call

src/sse.rs — SseEventStream::poll_next():
- Avoid std::mem::take + buffer re-creation on every chunk; extend
  utf8_buffer in place instead
- Determine valid UTF-8 length once, feed valid portion to parser,
  then efficiently drain consumed bytes (clear if fully consumed,
  otherwise keep only trailing incomplete sequence)
- Use memchr for ~4x faster newline scanning in SseParser::feed()
  vs str::find

src/extensions_js.rs — maybe_cjs_to_esm():
- Promote require() regex from per-call Regex::new() to static
  OnceLock<Regex> — avoids regex compilation on every CJS-to-ESM
  conversion call

Cargo.toml:
- Remove duplicate memchr = "2.7.6" entry (already declared as
  memchr = "2.7" in utilities section)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
